Merely taking the opposing stance, addressing a different sub-topic, or arguing for the other side is NOT enough. There must be a genuine conflict between the two specific claims.

Pair pro82:pro15
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro15] (pro) Civil waste and spent fuel storage is robust.

Pair pro82:pro15b
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro15b] (pro) Civil waste and spent fuel storage is often below ground level.

Pair pro82:pro4
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro4] (pro) They have to varying degrees either changed their public stance , or conceded that since nuclear power is virtually emission-free regarding CO2 , it merits at least grudging support as part of the response to increasing atmospheric CO2 levels and depletion of fossil fuels .

Pair pro82:pro95
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro95] (pro) Low levels of radiation comparable to those received naturally in some places are not harmful.

Pair pro82:pro36
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro36] (pro) If external costs are accounted, nuclear is very competitive.

Pair pro82:pro32
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro32] (pro) Also, if we know how to control atomic fusion, the same reactions that fuel the sun, we can have almost unlimited energy.

Pair pro82:pro29
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro29] (pro) Nuclear is much cheaper than wind or photovoltaic (PV).

Pair pro82:pro29b
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[pro29b] (pro) We have to bear the high costs of wind and solar for the sake of the environment.

Pair pro82:con36
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[con36] (con) Nuclear wastes (as, or in, spent fuel) are an unresolved problem.

Pair pro82:con27
  A=[pro82] (pro) Storage is safe and secure.
  B=[con27] (con) Uranium sources are just as finite as other fuel sources such as coal and natural gas.

direction    = exactly one of: A_ATTACKS_B / B_ATTACKS_A / NONE
confidence   = integer 0-100: probability that ANY attack exists between the two arguments

  {"pair": "id_a:id_b", "direction": "A_ATTACKS_B", "confidence": 85, "reason": "one sentence"},
  ...

  {"pair": "pro82:pro29", "direction": "NONE", "confidence": 0, "reason": "Cost comparison with wind/PV does not undermine the safety claim."},
  {"pair": "pro82:pro29b", "direction": "NONE", "confidence": 0, "reason": "Statement about wind/solar costs is unrelated to storage safety."},
